records.ety: [OE. tombe, toumbe, F. tombe, LL. tumba, fr. Gr. <?/ a tomb, grave; perhaps akin to L. tumulus a mound. Cf. Tumulus.]

defs: A pit in which the dead body of a human being is deposited; a grave; a sepulcher.
quotes: As one dead in the bottom of a tomb.

defs: A house or vault, formed wholly or partly in the earth, with walls and a roof, for the reception of the dead.
A monument erected to inclose the body and preserve the name and memory of the dead.
quotes: Hang her an epitaph upon her tomb.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Tombed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Tombing.]

defs: To place in a tomb; to bury; to inter; to entomb.
quotes: I tombed my brother that I might be blessed.
